description: Auto-Pilot Mode - The AI showpiece feature with two modes: 'Ask Permission' (AI plans the day, shows plan, waits for approval) and 'Full Auto' (AI does everything automatically then tells user what it did). Includes backend endpoints and frontend UI.
steps: STEP 1 - Backend: Create a new file backend/app/api/autopilot.py with a FastAPI APIRouter. Add two endpoints: POST /api/autopilot/plan (generates a day plan using scheduler+planner+priority agents but does NOT execute it) and POST /api/autopilot/execute (takes a plan and actually creates/moves events and tasks).

STEP 2 - Backend: The /api/autopilot/plan endpoint should: (a) authenticate with auth_token, (b) fetch current tasks via planner.list_tasks, (c) fetch today's calendar events via MCP list_events, (d) use PriorityAgent to rank tasks, (e) use Gemini to generate an optimized day plan that: blocks focus time, schedules tasks into free slots, adds buffers between meetings. Return the plan as JSON with structure: { plan_id: string, actions: [{ type: 'create_event'|'move_event'|'schedule_task', details: {...} }], summary: string }.

STEP 3 - Backend: The /api/autopilot/execute endpoint should: (a) authenticate, (b) accept a plan (list of actions), (c) execute each action by calling the appropriate MCP tools (create_event, update_event, etc.), (d) return a summary of what was done: { executed: number, failed: number, changes: [...] }.
STEP 4 - Backend: Register the autopilot router in backend/app/main.py with app.include_router(autopilot_router).
STEP 5 - Frontend API: Add functions in frontend/lib/api-extended.ts: fetchAutopilotPlan(authToken) and executeAutopilotPlan(authToken, planId, actions).

STEP 6 - Frontend: Create frontend/components/autopilot/AutoPilotPanel.tsx - A modal/panel component that: (a) Has a mode toggle at the top: 'Ask Permission' vs 'Full Auto', stored in localStorage key 'chronai-autopilot-mode'. (b) Shows a 'Plan My Day' button. (c) In 'Ask Permission' mode: clicking 'Plan My Day' calls fetchAutopilotPlan, shows the plan with accept/reject/adjust buttons. Each plan action shown as a card (create event at X, schedule task Y at Z, etc). User can toggle individual actions on/off. Accept calls executeAutopilotPlan with approved actions. (d) In 'Full Auto' mode: shows a WARNING modal first: 'This may change your current schedules in the calendar. Proceed?' On confirm, calls fetchAutopilotPlan then immediately executeAutopilotPlan. Shows a summary of all changes made.

STEP 7 - Frontend: Add the AutoPilot trigger to the dashboard home page (frontend/app/dashboard/page.tsx): Add a 'Plan My Day' button/card in the stats area or as a prominent action. Also add an Auto-Pilot mode toggle in the settings page (frontend/app/dashboard/settings/page.tsx) under AI Preferences section.

STEP 8 - Frontend: Style the AutoPilot panel with premium dark UI. Plan actions use colored icons: calendar events get CalendarIcon in accent-500, tasks get CheckSquare in warning-500, focus blocks get Timer in success-500. The warning for Full Auto mode uses a danger-500 AlertTriangle icon.
STEP 9 - Verify: Run frontend build + lint, and python3 -m py_compile on the backend files.
acceptance_criteria: npm run build succeeds with no errors
npm run lint shows no errors
POST /api/autopilot/plan endpoint exists and accepts auth_token
POST /api/autopilot/execute endpoint exists and accepts auth_token + actions
Frontend has AutoPilotPanel component with mode toggle
Ask Permission mode: shows plan, allows accept/reject/adjust
Full Auto mode: shows warning, then executes and shows summary
Plan My Day button exists on dashboard home page
Auto-Pilot mode toggle exists in settings
